entries.name: Hyakkaou Private Academy
entries.content: Hyakkaou Private Academy is an elite, 122-year-old institution for the children of Japan's most powerful families. Traditional academic and athletic metrics are irrelevant here; instead, the school is governed by a brutal gambling-based hierarchy. Success in high-stakes games determines a student's social standing, political influence, and treatment by their peers. The academy serves as a training ground for the next generation of leaders, preparing them to manipulate, exploit, and navigate a world where life is a series of bets.

entries.name: The Student Council
entries.content: The Student Council, led by the enigmatic Kirari Momobami, is the absolute authority at Hyakkaou. They enforce the gambling rules, collect mandatory 'donations' (effectively taxes), and manage the social hierarchy. Council members are the top gamblers in the school and wield immense power, including the ability to issue Life Plans. Kirari's leadership turned the school into a playground for the 'Hundred Devouring Families,' emphasizing a social Darwinist philosophy where only the strong survive.
entries.comment: The governing body of the school

entries.name: Housepets (Pochi & Mike)
entries.content: Students who fall into the bottom 100 contributors or accrue unpayable debts are stripped of their humanity and designated as Housepets. Males are called 'Pochi' (Fido) and females are 'Mike' (Mittens). They must wear a physical identification tag at all times and are subjected to systemic bullying, manual labor, and total social exclusion. A housepet's only recourse is the 'Public Match'—a one-time right to challenge any Student Council member to a game with stakes of their choosing, which cannot be refused.
entries.comment: The lower class of the school hierarchy

entries.name: Life Plan (Life Schedule)
entries.content: For students with insurmountable debt, the Student Council issues a 'Life Plan'. This small booklet dictates every detail of the student's future after graduation: whom they will marry, what career they will pursue, and how many children they will have. These plans are designed to maximize profit for the Council or the Momobami Clan. A Life Plan is a form of ultimate collateral and can be traded or won in a gamble, effectively giving one person total ownership over another's life.

entries.name: Gambling & Cheating
entries.content: Gambling at Hyakkaou is not just about luck; it is about psychological warfare, calculation, and deception. Cheating is an open secret—it is considered a valid tactical skill as long as one isn't caught. The school views cheating as a reflection of the 'rigged' nature of elite society. Yumeko Jabami is unique because she finds cheating 'honest'—it adds a layer of complexity to the game that makes the eventual reveal even more thrilling.
entries.comment: The philosophy of gambling in the setting
